dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal emphasizes longer-term trend-following (20-period specified) to capture abnormally large moves during regime shifts. Implemented as SMA crossover (20/50) as a standard trend-following mechanism. ATR scaled to >1.0 (in decimal form, representing volatility expansion consistent with 'abnormal price moves'). Entry requires both trend signal AND volatility expansion. Exit on trend reversal. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as the signal generates both long and short entries depending on regime direction.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal emphasizes longer-term trend-following (20-period specified) to capture abnormally large moves during regime shifts. Implemented as SMA crossover (20/50) as a standard trend-following mechanism. ATR scaled to >1.0 (in decimal form, representing volatility expansion consistent with 'abnormal price moves'). Entry requires both trend signal AND volatility expansion. Exit on trend reversal. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as the signal generates both long and short entries depending on regime direction.

source_rule_text: A trader should use longer-term trend-following indicators to reduce transaction costs and capture abnormally large price moves that occur during major market shifts, rather than relying on shorter-term indicators that generate more frequent signals.
